title: Base64 Decoder
shortDescription: Decode Base64 strings into readable text, bytes, or structured data from a clean input field.
metaDescription: Decode Base64 strings to readable text or binary output. Paste encoded data, inspect the result, and spot malformed input fast.
intro: Paste a Base64 string and the tool decodes it into readable text or bytes immediately.
overview:
Base64 is an encoding format, not encryption. This decoder converts Base64 input back into the original data whenever the text is valid and complete. If the input contains line breaks, URL-safe characters, or missing padding, the result may differ or fail depending on the source format.

Use it for text snippets, file payloads, JSON fragments, and encoded headers. If the decoded output is non-text data, the tool will still show the raw result or bytes rather than forcing unreadable characters into plain language.

For best results, paste the exact encoded value from the source system. Extra spaces, copied prefixes, or truncated strings are the most common reasons a Base64 decode does not match expectations.
howItWorks: Paste the Base64 value into the input field.
The tool decodes the content and shows the output immediately.
Copy the decoded text or inspect the raw output if the data is not plain text.

useCases.description: Decode values copied from logs, config files, or terminal output when the source system stores data in Base64.

faq.question: Why does my output look like symbols or unreadable text?
faq.answer: That usually means the decoded bytes are not plain text. The source may be an image, archive, or other binary payload, so the output should be treated as raw data.

faq.question: What happens if the string has missing padding?
faq.answer: Some Base64 values decode only when padding is restored. If the source trimmed the trailing '=' characters, the result may be rejected or need the exact original form.

faq.question: Can I decode URL-safe Base64 here?
faq.answer: URL-safe strings use different characters in some systems. If your value was copied from a URL token, make sure it is in the format expected by the source before decoding.

faq.question: Why is my result different from the original text?
faq.answer: A mismatch usually comes from copying extra spaces, losing characters, or decoding the wrong variant. Check the full string exactly as it appeared in the source.
